title: cyperus rivularis f. acutatus
intro: Cyperus rivularis f. acutatus, also known as the rivularis sedge or sharp-leaf papyrus, is a variant of the Cyperus rivularis plant, a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus rivularis f. acutatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and ease symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make poultices and ointments for topical application.
